title: Oldenlandia pulvinata
intro: Oldenlandia pulvinata is a perennial herb belonging to the family Rubiaceae, commonly found in various parts of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Oldenlandia pulvinata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often prepared into teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in combination with other herbs in formulae to address a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Oldenlandia pulvinata is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It belongs to the genus Oldenlandia, which is a group of small, herbaceous plants commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The classification of Oldenlandia pulvinata is further refined to the order Gentianales and the subclass Asteridae. This plant is a member of the large and diverse Rubiaceae family, which includes over 600 genera and 13,000 species. The classification of Oldenlandia pulvinata reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Gentianales order.
